issues_found: Source is a listing_page (navigation page), which per Core Rule 5 must be rejected
Source contains no substantive content - only event titles and timestamps
No published_at date provided, making temporal relevance impossible to verify
Cannot support any macro catalyst or market structure analysis
event_type set to 'unknown' is technically correct but reflects that no archiveable event content exists
Quality tier 'low' with score 1 is appropriate given zero substantive content
human_review_reason: Record correctly identifies all deficiencies and auto-rejection is clear per Rule 5 (listing page). The generated record accurately assessed itself as non-archiveable.

corrected_fields.summary: This is a Treasury.gov navigation page listing three webcast entries: an FSOC Open Session Meeting (March 25, 2026), a February 2026 Quarterly Refunding Webcast (February 4, 2026), and a Trump Accounts Summit (January 28, 2026). No substantive content, transcripts, or policy details are provided — only event titles and timestamps.
corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This page provides no archiveable research material. It functions solely as a navigation index to webcast events. Actual content (webcasts, slides, transcripts) would need to be accessed via individual links not provided in this source.
